Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

FAA Presses California for Records in Airport-Funding Dispute

California airport funding dispute draws new FAA scrutiny.

FAA's records request signals potential enforcement action against California, escalating a funding dispute that could reshape state airport operations and set precedent for federal oversight of airport finances.

La NASA anuncia la cobertura de la de la primera misión tripulada Artemis alrededor de la Luna

Diversos eventos previos al lanzamiento, del lanzamiento y de la misión Artemis II de la NASA alrededor de la Luna se transmitirán en línea. La agencia tiene como fecha objetivo no antes del miércoles 1 de abril para este vuelo de prueba, con una ventana de lanzamiento de dos horas que se abre a...

NASA's April 1 target for Artemis II lunar mission signals the agency's accelerating timeline to return humans to lunar orbit after decades of planning.

Equipped with DJI’s flagship 1-inch imaging, O4+ video transmission, and omnidirectional obstacle sensing, creators can now...

DJI's omnidirectional obstacle avoidance enables unprecedented safety for 360-degree FPV operations, reducing barrier-to-entry for immersive aerial content creation at scale.

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Organizations must document how they selected specific controls and justified acceptance of remaining risks to meet regulatory compliance under 14 CFR §5.55.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Southwest Airlines Transforms Austin-Bergstrom Airport, Unleashing A New Era For Travelers And Crew

The carrier has scheduled almost 3,500 departures from the Texan facility this month alone.

Southwest's 3,500 monthly departures from Austin signals a hub-building strategy that could reshape Texas aviation by concentrating point-to-point network capacity in a secondary market.

Military Mar 26, 2026

Pentagon eyes redirecting Ukraine’s Patriot missiles to Middle East

The Pentagon is considering whether to redirect air defense interceptor missiles originally earmarked for Ukraine to the Middle…...

Shifting air defense resources from Europe to the Middle East could strain global missile production capacity and delay Ukraine's air defense modernization amid competing geopolitical demands.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Virgin Australia named world’s best cabin crew for eighth year running

Virgin Australia has been named World’s Best Cabin Crew for the eighth consecutive year in the AirlineRatings.com World’s…...

Sustained cabin crew excellence creates measurable competitive differentiation, suggesting service quality investments yield quantifiable market recognition and likely customer loyalty advantages.

Safety Mar 26, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before incidents happen, fundamentally changing safety culture.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport Remains North America's Busiest By Total Passengers

With over 100 million passengers handled in 2025, ATL continues to dominate global rankings for the world's busiest airport.

ATL's sustained 100M+ passenger throughput demonstrates the critical infrastructure demands on hub airports that concentrate both domestic and international traffic flows.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA’s Hubble Detects First-Ever Spin Reversal of Tiny Comet

Astronomers using NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope have found evidence that the spinning of a small comet slowed and then reversed its direction of rotation, offering a dramatic example of how volatile activity can affect the spin and physical evolution of small bodies in the solar system. This is the...

Comet spin reversals demonstrate that outgassing forces can fundamentally alter trajectories of small celestial bodies, critical for aerospace mission planning near comets.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Hughes targets sovereign satcom demand with network control software

Company rolls out AI-enabled modem as militaries shift to hybrid networks...

Hughes' AI-enabled modem addresses military demand for resilient hybrid satellite-terrestrial networks, positioning commercial satcom vendors to capture defense modernization spending.

KSQL Noise Abatement Departure

The San Francisco Bay area is complicated airspace, and right in the middle of it is San Carlos Airport. San Carlos airport is surrounded by terrain, San Francisco International class bravo,...

San Carlos Airport's noise abatement procedures must navigate complex Bay Area airspace constraints and surrounding terrain, requiring pilots to execute precise departure profiles to maintain community relations.

Military Mar 26, 2026

Video Shows Russian Geran Drone Shot Down by Rifle-Armed Ukrainian Yak-52 Crew

A first-person point-of-view video shows the crew member in the rear seat of a Ukrainian Yak-52 shooting with a rifle at a Russian Geran drone, which later detonates in a powerful explosion. A video that emerged on Mar. 24, 2026, shows a Russian Geran-type One-Way Attack (OWA) drone exploding...

Ukrainian pilots are adapting Cold War-era trainer aircraft as makeshift interceptors, using small arms to engage low-flying drones where conventional air defense is unavailable.

See The Aviationist →

Military Mar 26, 2026

$147,000 Damage: Military KC-46A Tanker Rips Up 25 Foot Section Of Airport Tarmac

The continuous high temperature and pressure from the engine blast affected the integrity of the parking ramp, resulting in this damage.

Engine exhaust thermal degradation of airport infrastructure reveals potential maintenance liability issues for military aircraft operations at civilian facilities.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Here’s How Much Larger The Boeing 777-300ER’s Windows Are Compared To The Airbus A350

Did you know that the older Triple-Seven has larger windows than a 21st-century clean-sheet carbon-composite widebody?

Boeing's 777-300ER windows exceed A350 dimensions despite being nearly three decades older, demonstrating how cabin design priorities have shifted from passenger amenities to fuel efficiency and structural optimization.

See Simple Flying →

General Aviation Mar 26, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Regional airports like CID serve as crucial multi-use hubs supporting diverse aviation segments from flight training to commercial carriers, creating economic multiplier effects beyond typical general aviation.

AirAsia Philippines risks losing licenses over $14.5 million unpaid fees

The Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) has issued a final demand letter to AirAsia Philippines for…...

Regulatory enforcement against major carriers for fee non-compliance could trigger broader industry scrutiny of financial obligations and operating privileges across Southeast Asian markets.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Volotea closes smaller than anticipated funding round following strong results

Regional low-cost carrier Volotea has completed its latest capital increase round. The airline announced in September 2024 that…...

Volotea's decision to close a smaller funding round despite strong operational results suggests investor confidence in the airline's profitability trajectory may be reducing capital requirements.

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Toy Soldiers Bricking It: Danish Mix-Up Nearly Disassembles LegoLand Plane.

Back in September 2025, during a period of intense, unexplained unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) activity,...

The incident highlights critical supply chain vulnerabilities where industrial components can be misrouted to consumer manufacturers, potentially compromising aircraft safety protocols.

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation segments can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental impact, with research gaps remaining in implementation scalability.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

The Airline That Introduced Lie-Flat Business Class Seats To The World

Find out everything you need to know about the airlines that introduced lie-flat business class seats to the world.

Lie-flat seats fundamentally shifted business-class revenue models by justifying premium pricing through rest quality, enabling airlines to capture high-margin long-haul traffic from first-class.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Golden Dome and the velocity race: Why ground-based optics are the key to mission persistence

While often described as a moonshot for missile defense, the Golden Dome for America mission is in reality something even more challenging: an exercise in disciplined systems engineering under threat. Hypersonic glide vehicles, maneuvering ballistic missiles and complex decoys are compressing...

Ground-based optical tracking systems are becoming mission-critical for intercepting advanced hypersonic threats, shifting defense priorities toward persistent surveillance architecture rather than kinetic response speed alone.

See SpaceNews →

Business Aviation Mar 26, 2026

Bombardier delivers first Global 8000 to NetJets

Bombardier has handed over the first Global 8000 business jet to NetJets, marking the fleet launch customer’s entry…...

NetJets' adoption of the Global 8000 signals market confidence in next-generation ultra-long-range cabins as fractional operators prioritize premium cabin experience to justify higher fractional shares.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Up To 17-Hour Nonstop: Emirates' 10 Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Emirates is the world's second-largest long-haul operator, after United Airlines. In 2026, the carrier has an average of 134 daily long-haul passenger departures from its Dubai hub. Over half of the carrier's total flights fall into this category.

Emirates' dominance in ultra-long-haul routes reflects a strategic hub-and-spoke model where over 50% of operations are long-haul flights, enabling premium revenue concentration.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

United Airlines transforms devil’s chariot into premium CRJ-450

LOS ANGELES — Before the COVID-19 pandemic upended the airline industry, one-third of all United Airlines’ domestic US departures were on 50-seat regional jets. While that number has fallen to just 10% today, United isn’t resting on its laurels as demand for premium travel products remains high....

United's retrofit of 50-seat CRJ-450s with premium cabin configurations addresses the paradox of reduced regional jet capacity alongside persistent premium travel demand.

See Runway Girl Network →

Military Mar 26, 2026

South Korea rolls out first mass-production KF-21 Boramae

South Korea has rolled out the first mass-produced KF-21 Boramae fighter jet, completing a major production milestone for…...

South Korea's transition to mass production of the KF-21 Boramae signals the country's emergence as a credible indigenous fighter jet manufacturer and reduces regional dependency on foreign defense suppliers.

See AeroTime Hub →

Drones Mar 26, 2026

DJI Avata 360 global launch begins, US delayed again

DJI has kicked off the global rollout of the Avata 360, positioning it as its most immersive consumer drone yet. In many parts of the world, that means orders are either already live or pre-sales have begun. In the US, though, things are playing out a little differently, again. more…...

DJI's global expansion strategy now treats US market access as a secondary consideration, reflecting deepening regulatory fragmentation that forces drone manufacturers to prioritize international revenue streams.

See DroneDJ →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Up To 16-Hour Nonstop Flights: Etihad Airways' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Two of the routes are brand-new this year, while another started in 2025. Learn more here!

Etihad's expansion of ultra-long routes demonstrates how Middle Eastern hubs are capturing premium long-haul market share through aircraft efficiency and strategic positioning between Europe and Asia-Pacific.

Changi Airports International (CAI) announced it has signed a strategic cooperation agreement with Vietnam’s Sun Group to develop…...

Asian airport operators are expanding beyond traditional aviation services into integrated resort and leisure experiences to maximize revenue per passenger and compete for regional tourism share.

Military Mar 26, 2026

First Production KF-21 Boramae Rolled Out of Factory in South Korea

The first serial production KF-21 Boramae fighter jet has rolled out of the Korea Aerospace Industries (KAI) factory in a major milestone for the program. The first of 40 serial production KF-21 Boramae fighter jets has rolled out of the Korea Aerospace Industries (KAI) factory, marking a major...

South Korea's domestically-developed KF-21 fighter enters production phase, reducing the nation's reliance on foreign combat aircraft and establishing a new regional aerospace manufacturing capability.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA to test nuclear electric propulsion with 2028 mission to Mars

NASA’s new exploration strategy includes the rapid development of a nuclear-powered mission to Mars in 2028, leveraging hardware originally built for the lunar Gateway.

Repurposing lunar Gateway hardware accelerates Mars exploration timelines while reducing development costs for NASA's nuclear electric propulsion demonstration.

Airbus tests firefighting system linking drones, aircraft and ground crews

Integrated airborne and ground data sharing could significantly reduce coordination delays in emergency response operations, improving firefighting efficiency across multiple asset types.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

A350-900 vs 777-300ER: Who Wins on Range?

For more than two decades, the Boeing 777-300ER has been one of the most recognizable long-haul aircraft in the world. It has carried millions of passengers across continents, linking global hubs like Dubai, New York, Hong Kong, and Los Angeles with remarkable reliability. Airlines have long...

The A350-900's superior fuel efficiency enables comparable range to the 777-300ER while carrying more passengers, reshaping long-haul fleet economics for airlines planning their next-generation wide-body investments.
